title: Policy Optimizer - Manage Rules with Unused Applications
content:
This playbook helps identify and remove unused applications from security policy rules. If you have application-based security policy rules that allow a large number of applications, you can remove unused applications (applications never seen on the rules) from those rules to allow only applications actually seen in the rule’s traffic. This strengthens your security posture by reducing the attack surface.

Cortex XSOAR/XSIAM SOAR playbook from the Maciej Drobniuch and Cortex XSOAR pack (PANOSPolicyOptimizer, community-supported).

Inputs — slack_user: Slack user to notify about port based rules.; email_address: User email address to notify about port based rules.; auto_commit: Specifies whether you want to auto-commit the configuration for the PAN-OS policy changes automatically (Yes/No)..

Integrations/scripts used: IsIntegrationAvailable, pan-os-delete-rule, pan-os-po-unused-apps.

This strengthens your security posture by reducing the attack surface.

      description: |-

      playbookName: Policy Optimizer - Add Applications to Policy Rules
      type: playbook
      iscommand: false
      brand: ""
    nexttasks:
      '#none#':
      - "23"
    scriptarguments:
      rules_list:
        complex:
          root: PanOS.PolicyOptimizer.UnusedApps
          accessor: '@name'
    separatecontext: false
    loop:
      iscommand: false
      builtincondition:
      - - operator: isEqualString
          left:
            value:
              complex:
                root: RulesToEdit
                transformers:
                - operator: toLowerCase
            iscontext: true
          right:
            value:
              simple: "no"
      exitCondition: ""
      wait: 1
      max: 100
